title: Point rename_contract.sh's token-retrieval check at its new docs/installation.md home
action: tests/rename_contract.sh had drifted stale in five places, all traced to content the readme-rewrite commit (c7b7ea9) deliberately relocated out of README.md into docs/: retarget the token-retrieval guidance (line 44), canonical token path (line 45), chmod 600 guidance (line 46), and restart guidance (line 47) to docs/installation.md where that content now lives; retarget the source-build guidance (line 122) to docs/advanced/source-build.md. Separately, fix a real test-methodology bug in assert_after_preflight (lines 51-55): it took the FIRST match of a mutation pattern anywhere in install.sh, but the --uninstall early-exit branch (added by the unrelated, already-shipped cross-platform-install feature) shares three of these substrings (service stop, unit removal, daemon-reload) and sits before the systemd preflight check, so the first-match search was picking up that unrelated branch instead of the real post-preflight install-flow mutation. Confirmed by inspection that the real install-flow occurrences (lines 213, 215, 237) are correctly ordered after preflight (line 114) - this is a test false-positive, not a live ordering regression. Fixed by finding the first match strictly after preflight_line instead of the file's first match overall.

truths: Every retargeted assertion checks the file where its guidance now actually lives
assert_after_preflight finds a match after the preflight line rather than requiring the file's first match to be after it
The real install-flow mutation ordering (conflict-service-stop, legacy-unit removal, daemon-reload, all after the systemd preflight check) is still genuinely verified, not skipped

artifacts.substantive: five stale README-only assertions retargeted to their docs/ home, plus assert_after_preflight scoped to match after the preflight line instead of first-in-file

prohibitions: Do not touch any file other than tests/rename_contract.sh
Do not weaken any assertion's actual guarantee - only fix which occurrence/location it checks

trace.outcome: Retargeted 5 stale README-only assertions to their new docs/ home and fixed a first-match false-positive in assert_after_preflight caused by the --uninstall branch
